description: Comprehensive practice covering Dawkins Ch 10: sequences, convergence tests (divergence, integral, comparison, alternating, ratio, root), geometric/telescoping series, power series, Taylor/Maclaurin series, binomial series, and applications.

questions.question_text: Use the Comparison Test or Limit Comparison Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{3}{\sqrt{n} + 2} \) converges or diverges.
questions.answer_plain: Diverges by Limit Comparison with b_n = 3/\sqrt{n}

questions.question_text: Write the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} - \frac{3 \left(-1\right)^{n}}{n} \) after stripping the first 1 term(s).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=2 to inf of -3*(-1)**n/n

questions.question_text: Determine whether the geometric series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} -3 \left(\frac{3}{8}\right)^n \) converges or diverges. If it converges, find its sum.
Apply the Divergence Test to the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{4}{n^{4}} \). What can you conclude?
questions.answer_plain: Divergence Test is inconclusive (limit = 0). Another test is needed.

questions.question_text: Determine whether the geometric series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} 3 \left(- \frac{3}{7}\right)^n \) converges or diverges. If it converges, find its sum.
Rewrite the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{2}{n \left(n + 5\right)} \) so that the summation starts at \( n = 1 \).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=1 to inf of 2/((n - 1)*(n + 4))

questions.question_text: Rewrite the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{3 \left(-1\right)^{n}}{2 n + 1} \) so that the summation starts at \( n = 3 \).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=3 to inf of 3*(-1)**(n - 3)/(2*n - 5)

questions.question_text: Determine whether the geometric series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} -2 \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^n \) converges or diverges. If it converges, find its sum.
Use the Root Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} n^{- 2 n} \) converges or diverges.
Use the Ratio Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{n!}{\left(2 n\right)!} \) converges or diverges.
Apply the Divergence Test to the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} - \frac{1}{n} \). What can you conclude?
questions.answer_plain: Divergence Test is inconclusive (limit = 0). Another test is needed.

questions.question_text: Determine whether the geometric series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} 3 \left(\frac{3}{7}\right)^n \) converges or diverges. If it converges, find its sum.
Apply the Divergence Test to the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{4}{n^{\frac{3}{2}}} \). What can you conclude?
questions.answer_plain: Divergence Test is inconclusive (limit = 0). Another test is needed.

questions.question_text: Use the Integral Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=2}^{\infty} \frac{4}{n \log{\left(n \right)} \log{\left(\log{\left(n + 2 \right)} \right)}} \) converges or diverges.
Rewrite the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} - \frac{4 x^{n}}{n + 1} \) so that the summation starts at \( n = 3 \).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=3 to inf of -4*x**(n - 3)/(n - 2)

questions.question_text: Find the sum of the telescoping series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} - \frac{3}{\left(2 n - 1\right) \left(2 n + 1\right)} \). (Hint: use partial fractions.)
Use Maclaurin series to evaluate \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sin{\left(x \right)}}{x} \).
Determine whether the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{\left(-1\right)^{n + 1}}{n^{4}} \) is absolutely convergent, conditionally convergent, or divergent.
Determine whether the sequence \( a_n = \frac{2 \log{\left(n \right)}}{n} \) is increasing, decreasing, or not monotonic. Is it bounded?
Use the Root Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} 3^{- n} n^{2} \) converges or diverges.
List the first 5 terms of the sequence \( a_n = 2 n^{2} + n - 1 \).
Use the Ratio Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} 6^{- n} n^{2} \) converges or diverges.
Write the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{3}{n^{\frac{3}{2}}} \) after stripping the first 2 term(s).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=3 to inf of 3/n**(3/2)

questions.answer_plain: Diverges by the Integral Test (1 ln(n)/n)

questions.answer_latex: \text{Converges by Direct Comparison with } b_n = 2 e^{-n}
questions.answer_plain: Converges by Direct Comparison with b_n = 2 e^{-n}

questions.question_text: Use the Root Test to determine whether \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \left(\frac{n + 5}{3 n + 1}\right)^{n} \) converges or diverges.
For the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} - 4^{- n} n \), compute the first three partial sums \( S_1, S_2, S_3 \).
Write the series \( \displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} - \frac{2 \cdot 2^{n}}{n!} \) after stripping the first 1 term(s).
questions.answer_plain: sum from n=2 to inf of -2*2**n/factorial(n)
